items.q: Where do object pronouns go in 'I see him' (lo veo)?
items.why: Students apply English word order. Spanish places object pronouns BEFORE conjugated verbs. They can attach to infinitives, gerunds, and positive commands - but not after a conjugated verb in normal sentences.

items.q: Why does 'qué' have an accent in '¿Qué quieres?' but not always elsewhere?
items.correct: The accent distinguishes interrogative/exclamative 'qué' from relative 'que'.
items.why: Students see two spellings of the 'same word'. Spanish uses written accents to distinguish homophones with different functions: qué (question/exclamation) vs que (that/which - relative).

items.q: How do you say 'I love films' in Spanish?
items.why: Students translate 'amar' (which is for people/love) or forget gustar/encantar structure. ENCANTAR works like gustar - backwards. Subject = películas (plural), so verb = ENCANTAN. 'Me encanta el cine' (singular subject) vs 'me encantan las películas' (plural).
Correct answer: Nahuatl. “Quechua” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Where is Guarani an official language?
items.why: It's Paraguay. “Argentina” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Approximate number of indigenous languages recognised in Mexico?
items.why: The real figure is 68. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
